title: Least Privilege — Excessive Cloud IAM Permissions

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

spl:
index=cloud sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail earliest=-7d eventName IN ("AttachUserPolicy","PutUserPolicy","AttachRolePolicy")
| spath path=eventSource output=eventSource
| spath path=requestParameters output=requestParameters
| search requestParameters="*AdministratorAccess*" OR requestParameters="*IAMFullAccess*"
| stats count by userName, eventName, requestParameters, aws_account_id
value: Reviews AWS CloudTrail `AttachUserPolicy` events for high-risk managed policies supporting AC-6 least privilege.
implementation: (1) Use `spath` or JSON KV mode consistently; (2) maintain deny-list of risky policy ARNs; (3) auto-ticket attachments; (4) quarterly access review export.
visualization: Table (who attached what), Time chart (high-risk grants), Bar chart (by account)
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
